div.div: Understand why the OSI model exists
Know the function of each layer
Map real protocols/devices to each layer
NOT the application itself (like Chrome), but the protocols apps use
Think: "What does the user want to do?"
Packets are the PDU (Protocol Data Unit)

tr.td: A router forwards a packet to another network
A switch learns a MAC address
You type google.com in your browser
TCP ensures your download isn't corrupted
A cable connects your PC to a switch

div.div:
1.
At which layer does a switch primarily operate?
Click for answer Layer 2 — Data Link (uses MAC addresses)

2.
What is the PDU called at Layer 4?
Click for answer Segment (TCP) or Datagram (UDP)

3.
Which layer handles encryption?
Click for answer Layer 6 — Presentation (though modern encryption often happens at Layer 7 with TLS/SSL)
🌐 Day 1: The OSI Model — 7 Layers of Networking
